ops.title: Getaway skill redesign: balance-bias, seat quality ranking, transfer-first affordability
ops.body:
# Getaway ranking and affordability: the deliberate decisions

Commit 9a5fc1f6b1f2 encodes five interrelated design decisions. Each was chosen over a rejected alternative — do not revert one without understanding why it won.

## 1. Balances bias, never gate

A zero or missing balance never removes a program from a sweep or shortlist. Rejected alternative: filtering sweeps to funded programs — it hides options one bank transfer could fund. Per-program sweeps order funded-first only so a quota-capped pass spends calls on likelier-bookable options. `sources` narrows only on an explicit user ask, never derived from balances.

## 2. `barely` seats soft-demote but stay ranked

Seat quality (skills/getaway/seat-quality.md) uses four verdicts; ONLY the literal `barely` verdict (yin-yang 2-3-2/2-4-2 like old BA Club World, 7-across flats, anything angled) demotes, and it soft-demotes — sinks below every true lie-flat but stays on the board with a warning. Rejected alternatives: hard-dropping (hides the only option in a thin window) and near-tie-only tiebreak (a cheap bad seat would still win). `verify` and `unknown` rank NEUTRAL — never demote what the table doesn't condemn. Sort precedence is (soft airline avoid, `barely`, mileage): the avoid list outranks the seat, deliberately. The verdict rates the LONGEST business-cabin segment; positioning legs and economy connectors never rate a trip. Suite-vs-solid near-tie promotion is a presentation-layer prose contract (like the affordability tiebreak), intentionally absent from the workflow comparator.

## 3. Seat classification happens mid-Expand, behind a buffer

Aircraft type exists only in `/trips/{id}` segments, never on availability rows — so ranking by product requires expanding MORE candidates than final finalists. Business asks expand ~1.5× `maxFinalists` (capped 12); that extra quota is the acknowledged price of never ranking a yin-yang seat over a flat bed. The low-quota path (`Math.min(maxFinalists, 3, minQuota)`) drops the buffer FIRST. The Verify phase (WebSearch, zero quota) resolves Verify-marked and table-absent products BEFORE the re-rank truncates, so a resolved `barely` never displaces a true flat. Non-business cabins are fully inert: same ordering as before.

## 4. Transfer-first affordability, annotation not exclusion

Unfunded finalists keep their shortlist spot and gain an annotation (covered / transfer suggestion / buy estimate). Transfers precede cash buy suggestions; buying is only suggested for small residual shortfalls when plausibly good value — a judgment call, no numeric threshold. Ranking stays mileage-first; funding breaks only near-ties (~10–15%). Transfers move in fixed blocks (Chase 1,000-point increments, eligible-card required) — round up before claiming coverage.

## 5. QAF-first Africa strategy

Africa sweeps ride the undocumented `QAF` pseudo-code on `/search` (verified live 2026-07-12); the per-program `availability --dest-region Africa` sweep is the fallback, covering all programs funded-first, never trimmed to funded ones.

## Gotchas that guard these decisions

- seats.aero `AircraftName` identifies the type, never the sub-config — that gap is exactly what seat-quality.md's Verify column covers (marked only when a mix crosses the demotion line or spans ≥2 tiers).
- The trip-expansion jq must null-guard the `best` projection (`min_by` on an empty selection is null; iterating `.AvailabilitySegments[]` on null crashes jq) and filter segments on `.Cabin` before `max_by(.Distance)`.
- `TRIP_SCHEMA.longhaul` is `['string', 'null']` — a business trip whose segments all ride economy legitimately returns null.
- Pack schemas are closed: neither affordability nor seat verdicts ride as extra fields on `getaway.*` blocks; both go in the built-in `section` block beside the picker.
ops.when: Before modifying affordability logic, seat quality ranking, program selection, or balance handling in the getaway skill.
